Remember people buy on emotion. You create the logical case first. I say to you, listen, Kodak earns four bucks a share. The industry is trading at 20 times earnings right now. Do math. Four times 20 is 80 bucks. Right now the stock is 42. Take the company. Let's look at the breakup value. Take it, break it up, sell it off in small chunks. The breakup value is $130 a share. Right now the stock is 42. Let me ask you a question, sir. What do you think is going to happen to the price of the stock after litigation sales and institutions come pouring back into the stock? Where do you think it's going to go? Well, obviously it's going hard. Say, exactly. That's a logical case. You say, you get it? You do math? You do asset values? That's a logical case. Make that case. They'll say, mm-hmm. Let me call you back. They won't buy. But if you make that logical case to someone, and then after, they're freed up. See, what happens is the logical brain is almost the bullshit detector. This doesn't make sense. It's looking for bullshit. Once you satisfy the logical brain, you then free them up to be moved emotionally. Everyone get that process? What you do is you need to ask for permission to ask questions. You say, I'm going to ask you a couple of quick questions so I don't waste your time. A couple of quick questions so I can best serve you. You have a transition you ask for permission to ask questions. You never just start asking questions. So you ask for permission. Right there. One of the most important things here when you're actually going to bounce is you've got to be using a script for your questions. Don't just be making up your questions really nearly. Every single industry, whether I'm not I'm sure what product you're selling, the certain core things that you need to know about a client. Make sure that your questions are well scripted out. I'm going to give you sample questions as part of the system. When you actually ask questions, you want to start off with big picture questions. You want to move from the general to the specific. You go from the general to the specifics. You want to, at some level, the deepest level, to say, what are their whys? They have children. Something really matters. Certainty is not forever. It wanes very quickly. Here's what happens. I can get you to a point of certainty. Boom, you're at a 10 for about a minute and a half. And then the second later, you drop back down to an eight. Remember I said in the beginning on day one, I said, what you want to do is you want to corral a 10, 10, 10 in a single moment in time and ask for the order. A single moment in time. Guess what? At 4.12 in the afternoon, you've got someone to attend on the company. At 4.14, they're at 8 and a half. In a minute, they already forgot all the great things you said, which is why you're constantly looping back and reminding them that fundamentally logically, you keep satisfying the logical mind a little bit. But as you move down the line, what happens is this. You'll notice in the beginning, it's poor logic in the company. And the end, it's all really you, your company, in future pacing, and moves much more on the emotional level. It's mostly about you. But you're always throwing a little bit about the company to bring them, button them back up to a 10. It's like a safety check to get someone back up to a 10 for certainty. Does that make sense to everyone? Certainty wanes. Like right now, who is certain? This is make-sourced. Raise your hand. This makes sense. Everybody, right? Trust me, in four hours, you'll say, oh, it's great. But I'm not quite certain anymore. Not because I did anything wrong. That's how we are. We never stay very certain about something for very long until it becomes a conviction. Then once you're really fricking sure from the balls up, if you're a guy, and I won't say that, but if you're really sure, then no one could sway you. Like right now, no one could sway me if there's a better way to sell stock in this company. I'm not going to sway me if there's a better way to sell stock in this because I know it is not. But someone that's less certain, they just heard about it. They just say, you're certain now, and then in two hours, you're just not quite as certain. So what I do to make sure, I don't want to go through all of this and then say, oh, shit, he fell back to an 8 and 1 1 half. So I throw in a bit about the company to make sure he's back being certain. Does that make sense? This is where the sale begins when he says what? Let me think about it. Let me speak to my wife. Let me call you back. Let me check with my accountant. It's the wrong time of the year. The market sucks right now. It's ground on, it's leap year. It's New Year's Eve. It's Christmas. It's tax time. God knows what. It's summertime. I'm going on vacation right now. I got to check with my father. All the fricking stalls in the world. What he won't say is, it's a good idea, Joe. I don't trust you because you're a fricking scam prank. My mom told me when I was five years old. That's not coming out. And that's the truth. The truth is he's not buying from you. A, of course he's probably not certain yet about Kodak. Well, maybe he was a great pitch you gave him. But he's certainly skeptical about you and your company at this point, depending on how well known your company is. Maybe for the McQuarrie or Big A House, you have maybe some of his worst credibility or anything. What's in the news these days is a big, you know, never know anymore. Right? You used to be, you call for a big wire house and you're in good shape, not anymore, right? But the point is this, is that you're expecting him to say one of those stalls. And you are saying, ah, just as expected. And you utter your magical first straight line pattern, which is what? I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? And of course we know there's how many things he could say. What? Three. He could say, oh my god, yeah, I fucking love it. Usually he doesn't say that. Just pretend this is like the water line in the ocean. Here's your water line. The world's worst artist. And when you're on the phone with a client running a straight line pattern, and he's saying no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, you're not like this. It's not going like that. What's happening is this. This is the water line here, right? Water line. This is essentially success. When he goes above the water line and you see it, he says yes. When he's saying no, he's below the water line. What's happening is this. As you're going, you're getting closer and closer and closer and closer and right. So you don't see the result yet. The same way a guy who's working to build his business, he might not be making money yet, but he's making massive progress below the water line. And all of a sudden, that last pinch of certainty, the guy says yes, and boom, you're above the water. He said, shit, the guy just said yes. No, he didn't. He was saying yes the whole time. He was getting close. Yes, yes, yes, to certain parts of certainty. He just wasn't there on all three tens, or he had to lower his action threshold. So what happens is this. If this is the three tens, then lowering someone's action threshold will be what? You'd be essentially lowering the water line. So you could actually increase his certainty or lower the water line and make it easier for the guy to say yes. But let me tell you something. When you are running a straight line that's, let's say, minute one and minute 60, and he closes here at minute 40, you don't think you're making progress between one and 40. You're making massive progress. It's just below the water line. This is why great salespeople understand that it takes three, four, or five no's to actually get to the yes. What no is, it's almost a euphemism for saying what? No means I am not certain. That's all it means. When someone says, let me call you back, let me think about it, I want to talk to my wife, or just plain old no, it just means I am not certain. In sales, the ultimate equation when you're closing is this. It's called energy in, energy in equal benefits out. Benefits out. Energy in versus benefits out. Meaning if you lay out a closing scenario where someone has to expend lots of energy, they have to expend lots of energy to get only a few benefits, you think they're going to buy? Yes or no? No, they won't buy. Lots of energy in, few benefits out, let me think about it. Very little energy in, very little energy in. Massive benefits out. Will they buy? They very well might, they'll tend to buy. Massive benefits out, massive benefits received, very little energy put in. You say to someone, what I'd like you to do is this, pick up a block of 10,000 shares of Kodak. It's a cash outlay of $420,000. You need to go down to your bank right now and wire the money. Then we'll open up an account. I'm going to send you the information, and you need to fill out the entire account form here tonight. You need to have it typed, please type it, don't write it. I want you to type the account form up please, and then put it in with a copy of your license. Also need your passport as well. As you can go down to the FedEx, so make sure it gets there by five o'clock and deliver that. So it comes to about 10.30, open up the account, and believe me, you'll be very, very impressed. What do you say? Now that's an exaggerated form of the worst close ever, where you say, let me tell you how difficult it is to get started here. You have to fill out this long-winded account form. You got to make copies of all your personal details. I want you to go down to the bank, get a certified check, run the FedEx, drop it in the mailbox, run back home, and pick up that, and what's the worst, what's the most energy of all? That's a lot of energy, right? They got to run around and do shit. What's the most energy of all? 10,000 shares of Kodak. 420,000 bucks, what does that represent? Lots of store energy. How long did they have to work to make 420 grand? Maybe a fucking year. So I want you to take one year of energy that you have stored up and give it to me? For what? Where are the benefits? Well, maybe I might if you tell me about the amazing benefits and you make it really, really easy for me to engage in the transaction, which is why you say, getting started, write these words down. Getting started is very, very simple. And what happens with the straight line, and I'll tell you what happened with my firm now, is once I talk this to these kids, okay, it ignited a force in them, because when you have this ability, it's almost like it wakes up something inside you, because what changes is not only your ability to influence other people, it's about to influence who? Yourself. You start feeling completely different about yourself. And these kids went out there and they just, they raised their standards, and I would give meetings every single day, and I would crack their limiting beliefs. They would come in, their parents never told them to be rich, right? They thought they'd be working in 7-Eleven, these kids. I would say, in the moment you walk through this door and you sit down behind this desk and you say the words I teach you, everything in the past falls off. You become as powerful as the most powerful CEO in America. You pick up that phone as a weapon, you're a terrorist, and then I said, I went wild, okay? And I said some stuff I shouldn't have said, but the point is, is that, the point is, I'm a little bit older and wiser now, okay? But what I did is the first thing I did is I cracked their limiting beliefs. And these kids went out there and achieved things, and I inserted what was called a motivation strategy, which I do now more elegantly, and I know it, I didn't, unconsciously, I showed them how to motivate themselves using anchors like pain and pleasure, and we've all heard of that before, but I took it to a much higher level. I did it at a much higher level and boom, it worked. Ultimately, the mistake I made was very simple. I'll tell you what it was. The system was incredibly effective to the point where it's still, today, it's still used all over Wall Street. Every big firm uses a straight line. If you look at my wall on Facebook, everyone's a straight line, this, the, the, the whole thing. I didn't even want to teach it. When I first started going, I taught everything but the straight line because I thought, oh, it's, people don't commit bad things with it. It's like, pull out my own limiting belief. The system's beautiful and elegant. The problem was is that I, myself, at the age of 26, became greedy, and that's it. At the heart of my problems, I would like to say it was drugs that made me do things that I went to jail for 22 months because I manipulated some stocks, and I deserved to go to jail for that because I took this unbelievable, beautiful system that it works in any business, and I bastardized it by allowing them to use it to convince people to do shit they shouldn't do. I could have, my average income was 50, 60 million a year in my 20s. I could have made 30 million legitimately. 95% of what I did was strictly legit. It was the 5% that took me off the reservation and I paid the ultimate price for it. The idea is that greed is not good, but the belief that Gordon Deco inserted in me, my ass, when I saw he's on stage, greed is good, remember the movie, right? Greed is good. I was like, fuck, greed is good. I thought that was true, it's not. It's ambition, that's good. Greed doesn't cut through and clarify anything. Ambition cuts through, ambition clarifies to have a vision for a better future, not just for you, for your family, for the people you love. That's what I was missing. So I took the system that I just made as much as I could, as quick as I could, and I paid the price. Ultimately, I'm fortunate. I wrote a book, I got lucky. I wrote this book and it got picked up all over the world, the cap year goes, oh, great, great, great, great, great. My point is this, okay, is that if you can look at my life in any way, I want you to be able to pick up the great stuff from it, and this stuff, which is amazing, but learn from the bad too, is that when you develop this power, and you can do this stuff and really use it, only use it to help people get the things they need and to make decisions they should be making. Don't go off the reservation.
